trivia_bullets.text: Alley Assailant is a common Vampire Rogue from Murders at Karlov Manor (released February 9, 2024), set on the plane of Ravnica. The set re-frames Ravnica as a crime-fiction noir stage — a murder mystery rather than guild politics — with the Ravnican Agency of Magicological Investigations (R.A.M.I.) as the investigative body. [Scryfall; Wizards.com: Murders at Karlov Manor Mechanics]

Alley Assailant uses the Disguise mechanic — a variation on Morph introduced in MKM. A Disguise creature can be cast face down for {3} as a generic 2/2 with Ward 2, then flipped face up for its Disguise cost ({4}{B}{B}) at any time. The Ward 2 addition over classic Morph makes face-down creatures harder to remove, serving the set's theme of suspects hiding in plain sight. Scryfall confirms Alley Assailant has a mechanical relationship with 'A Mysterious Creature' (the face-down token card). [Scryfall; Wizards.com: 'Murders at Karlov Manor Mechanics'; CoolStuffInc: Jim Davis 'The Mechanics of Murders at Karlov Manor']
sources: CoolStuffInc: Jim Davis, 'The Mechanics of Murders at Karlov Manor'

trivia_bullets.text: Alley Assailant is a 3/3 that enters tapped and has an on-flip life-drain effect (opponent loses 3 life, you gain 3 life). Its EDHREC rank is 23,912 — low Commander adoption, consistent with face-down flip creatures being slow in multiplayer. It is legal in Standard, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, Pauper, and Commander as of 2024. [Scryfall; EDHREC]
Artist Warren Mahy has illustrated numerous MTG cards across multiple sets. No artist interview specifically addressing Alley Assailant was found in this research pass. [Scryfall]
